prompt: Operator: tiled matrix multiplication c = a @ b (float16 inputs, float32 output via cube accumulator). Input shapes: (M=K=N=16) and (M=32, K=16, N=32). Output shape: [M, N]. Dtype: float16 inputs; float32 output (cube accumulator). Layout: contiguous. Axis: -1 (K axis, contracted). Tiling: block_grid; inside @asc2.jit, use asc2.tensor for a/b/c and asc2.load with location=asc2.TileLocation.L0A for the left operand and asc2.TileLocation.L0B for the right operand, then compute `a_tile @ b_tile` (or asc2.matmul), then asc2.store the float32 result; tile sizes must be multiples of 16; pick small shapes that fit L0A/L0B (each ≤ 64 KiB) — start with m=k=n=16, core_num=1, m_tile=n_tile=16; matmul requires the cube unit on Ascend950PR_9599 (the only CI platform). Tail: aligned_only — shapes must be 16-multiples; unsupported_regimes lists non_16_multiple_shapes, k_tiled, abl1_full / al1_full / bl1_full. Accumulator: float32 (cube accumulator, mandatory). Tolerance: atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2 against c_ref = a.to(torch.float32) @ b.to(torch.float32); verify with torch.testing.assert_close(c, c_ref, atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2); host inputs MUST be torch.Tensor (a, b dtype=torch.float16; c zero-init torch.float32) because the C310 simulator silently produces zeros for matmul fed numpy arrays. Platform: Ascend950PR_9599. Build/run: python3.10 kernel.py -r Model -v Ascend950PR_9599. Evidence: kernel.py (skills-on adds design.md + verification.md).
